title: cerastium papuanum subsp. papuanum
intro: Cerastium papuanum subsp. papuanum, a plant species also known as New Guinea chickweed, is a type of flowering plant native to New Guinea, belonging to the carnation family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cerastium papuanum subsp. papuanum include reducing inflammation and treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as aiding in wound healing and soothing sore throats. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
